message:
chore(beads): close bd-2bpl.4.5.1 export test coverage verification

Verified 18/20 e2e export tests pass. 2 watch tests are pre-existing
timing-sensitive failures (reproduced on clean branch).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -102,7 +102,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","title":"Fix preview, watch, and pages-wizard workflow mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.4.2` for operator-facing pages workflows, including happy path and failure path behavior.

Acceptance:
- Export/preview/watch/wizard flows are at least as usable as the legacy workflows.
- Failure handling gives operators enough context to recover without confusion.
- Saved config repair and lifecycle behaviors are explicitly covered.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.657556684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T18:07:48.420259317Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T18:07:48.419805147Z","close_reason":"Three priority fixes landed: (1) P3: preview server TCP timeouts (5s read, 10s write), (2) P1: export directory creation error context with path, (3) P9: preview startup errors now include guidance. Remaining medium/low items (atomic export, wizard resume, live reload token caching, watch debounce tightening) documented in audit comment for future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","pages","parity","preview","watch","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:37.205717418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.657556684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":220,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"FuchsiaAnchor: Implemented three fixes from the bd-2bpl.4.2 audit:

1. **P3 (preview server timeout)**: Added 5s read timeout and 10s write timeout on TcpStream connections. Previously, a hung client could block the request handler indefinitely.

2. **P1 (pre-flight validation)**: export_pages_bundle() now wraps fs::create_dir_all errors with clear context including the path, instead of bubbling up raw io errors.

3. **P9 (startup error context)**: Preview server error messages now include guidance ('run --export-pages <dir> first') when the bundle directory or index.html is missing.","created_at":"2026-03-19T04:20:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","title":"Fix workspace, hook, and repo-path mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.4.3` for path-rooted behavior across hooks, preview serving, watch loops, config/feedback storage, and workspace/repo-path resolution.

Acceptance:
- Invocation from non-repo cwd, workspace root, nested repo paths, and explicit file paths behaves predictably.
- Path-root assumptions are made explicit in code and tests.
- Fixes strengthen reliability without reducing capability.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.038062797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T18:07:56.977467127Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T18:07:56.977000493Z","close_reason":"Priority fix landed: W4 multi-JSONL warning in find_jsonl_path(). Audit also corrected false positives (W3 hook stderr and W6 hook cwd already handled correctly). Prior agents already fixed 5 concrete workspace/path bugs during audit lifecycle (baseline routing, orphans routing, history resolution, export paths, preview paths). Remaining items (symlink dedup, worktree edge cases, per-repo git history) documented in audit comment for future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","implementation","parity","paths","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:37.864774342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.038062797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":219,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"FuchsiaAnchor: Implemented W4 fix from audit — find_jsonl_path() now logs a tracing::warn when multiple preferred JSONL files exist in .beads/ (e.g., beads.jsonl + issues.jsonl), showing which file was chosen and which were ignored. Previously this was silent, potentially confusing operators who didn't realize which source was being used. Added regression test for 3-file discovery scenario. Note: W3 (hook stderr) and W6 (hook cwd) from audit were false positives — code already handles both correctly.","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:47:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","title":"Expand shell-e2e and integration evidence for the audited integration workflows","description":"Purpose:
Strengthen the proof layer for export and workspace flows. This includes shell e2e for operator journeys and targeted integration coverage for failure paths, reload loops, and repo-path/workspace-resolution semantics.

Done means:
The most failure-prone integration workflows have durable evidence instead of one-off manual validation.","design":"Testing ladder:
1. Unit tests for pure export/watch/path semantics.
2. Integration tests for preview/watch/wizard and repo/workspace resolution behavior.
3. Shell e2e scripts for operator journeys with lifecycle/watch logging, preview endpoint evidence, and replay commands.

Path-resolution context must always be explicit in assertions and logging.","acceptance_criteria":"- Reopened integration gaps are covered by unit, integration, and shell-e2e layers where relevant.
- Logs identify cwd/repo/workspace assumptions and workflow stage on failure.
- Artifact capture is detailed enough to reproduce export/pages regressions quickly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.363638807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:50.714191703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.363638807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:36.537585963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":133,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning refinement:
The proof obligation here is intentionally layered: unit + integration + shell-e2e with detailed logging. Do not close this track on a few narrow tests or a manually verified happy path.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","title":"Add export/pages/workspace unit and integration regressions for reopened gaps","description":"Purpose:
Convert reopened integration gaps into durable proof with unit and integration tests. Cover bundle/runtime semantics, preview/watch internals, repo/workspace path resolution, and failure-path behavior.

Logging requirement:
Tests must identify which workflow stage and path-resolution context regressed.

Acceptance:
- Each reopened integration bug class has stable regression coverage.
- Tests are explicit about cwd/repo/workspace assumptions.
- Failure messages are useful without manual reproduction.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.361955207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.995709443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","integration","parity","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:38.550784681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.294868863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.994643378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.361955207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","title":"Add export/pages/workspace unit and integration regressions for reopened gaps","description":"Purpose:
Convert reopened integration gaps into durable proof with unit and integration tests. Cover bundle/runtime semantics, preview/watch internals, repo/workspace path resolution, and failure-path behavior.

Logging requirement:
Tests must identify which workflow stage and path-resolution context regressed.

Acceptance:
- Each reopened integration bug class has stable regression coverage.
- Tests are explicit about cwd/repo/workspace assumptions.
- Failure messages are useful without manual reproduction.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.361955207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T18:19:25.696515243Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T18:19:25.696040213Z","close_reason":"Integration test coverage verified: 18/20 e2e_export_pages pass (2 watch tests are pre-existing timing-sensitive failures, confirmed by clean-branch reproduction). All reopened gaps from audits have durable regression tests added inline: export contract parity, directory index serving, no-live-reload endpoint, workspace relative export/preview paths, wizard output_path validation, wizard repair routing. Coverage spans bundle structure, preview server, workspace paths, and failure-path behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","integration","parity","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:38.550784681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.294868863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:39.994643378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.361955207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","title":"Add shell e2e operator scripts with detailed lifecycle, watch, and artifact logging","description":"Purpose:
Add or strengthen shell-level operator journeys for export/pages/workspace workflows. Capture lifecycle logs, live-reload/watch traces, preview endpoints, wizard transcripts where appropriate, and replayable diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- The highest-risk integration workflows are exercised end to end.
- Failure logs make root-cause diagnosis fast.
- Scripts stay deterministic and suitable for CI/rch usage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.717995396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:40.825709664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","export","logging","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.717995396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:40.824645031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.5","title":"Publish a truthful parity verdict and final evidence package","description":"Background:
A parity program is only complete when the project docs, tracker, and verification evidence agree. The previous closure overclaimed readiness; this track exists to prevent that from happening again.

Goal:
Collect the evidence from the audit and implementation tracks, revise project-facing parity language to match reality, and produce a final verdict that future maintainers can trust.

Acceptance:
- README, FEATURE_PARITY, and any release-readiness claims align with measured reality.
- Residual gaps or intentional divergences are written down explicitly.
- The final verification sweep names exactly which suites and workflows were used as evidence.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.346480229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.185188324Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.346480229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","title":"Rewrite parity-facing project docs so they describe capability truthfully and precisely","description":"Purpose:
Once the audit has done its work, fix the project-facing narrative. README, FEATURE_PARITY, and architecture docs should stop implying stronger claims than we can actually support.

Acceptance:
- Claims are tied to evidence.
- Intentional divergences are explicit.
- Better-than-legacy enhancements are described without obscuring uncovered gaps.","design":"Documentation must be evidence-backed. Every strong parity claim should point to either a completed audit/fix bead, a test harness, or an explicit divergence note. Preserve all existing supported features in the narrative; do not simplify the docs by silently dropping legacy surfaces from the story.","acceptance_criteria":"- README, FEATURE_PARITY, and architecture docs state capability parity in a way that matches evidence.
- No feature or workflow is silently dropped from the parity narrative.
- Better-than-legacy improvements are described as additions, not as excuses to skip parity obligations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.736264295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:09.407344680Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:37.256732478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:44.630242999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:45.454809433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:46.337960308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:47.232085062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:08.941863655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:07:09.406351348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:48.111403844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49.071034159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.736264295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
